summary: The AST/audit additions still have governance-grade bypasses: path scoping is not honored reliably, compile-fix scope has an overbroad carve-out, and audit-chain verification tolerates post-chain unchained lines.

findings.title: AST validators ignore --paths for three exception types
findings.evidence: mech-sig-prop-check discards parsed flags (`_ = parseFlags`) and iterates every FileDiff; compile-fix-scope-check and schema-predicate-check also iterate all parsed diff files without filtering by a.paths. A batch edit with separate approved per-file exceptions can be denied by the wrong exception's AST check, or validated against unrelated files.
findings.recommendation: Filter FileDiffs to the requested --paths before validating, and add a multi-file test with two approved exceptions whose scopes/symbols differ.

findings.title: Path matching still collides on same basenames
findings.evidence: pathMatches accepts any diff path whose suffix is '/' + filepath.Base(target). For target '/tmp/a/x_test.go', diff path 'b/x_test.go' matches even though it is a different file. import-block-check uses this matcher, so same-basename files can be validated against the wrong on-disk source.
findings.recommendation: Normalize both diff paths and target paths relative to the project/root path and require exact relative-path equality; only use basename matching when there is exactly one unambiguous candidate.

findings.title: compile-fix scope carve-out permits off-scope identifier and literal changes
findings.evidence: When a hunk has one scoped-symbol change, any later line without the scoped symbol is accepted if isArgContinuation returns true. isArgContinuation allows IDENT, BasicLit, and punctuation and only rejects open delimiters/keywords, so lines like 'DangerousValue,' or '"prod",' can ride along in the same hunk without referencing the approved symbol.
findings.recommendation: Limit the carve-out to punctuation-only closers, or prove nesting inside the same scoped call before allowing argument-continuation lines without the scoped symbol.

findings.title: Audit verifier treats missing prev_sha as legacy even after the chain has started
findings.evidence: Any line without prev_sha is warned and accepted, then prev_line is updated. That is only safe for a pre-v1.8 prefix. A tampered final v1.8 audit line can remove prev_sha and pass verification because there is no subsequent line to force a mismatch.
findings.recommendation: Track whether a prev_sha-bearing line has been seen; tolerate missing prev_sha only before the chain starts, and fail closed for missing prev_sha after that point.
